readme: Gold key for the SAR Critic rubric eval (Phase 4). For each subject (selected by seed-stable role, not a hard-coded uid), 'expect_covered' is the set of FinCEN-rubric elements the case's evidence genuinely supports -- i.e. the coverage a correct, grounded SAR should reach. This is a SEPARATE committed key (like tests/data/advisory_gold.json) so it never perturbs the seeded generator. The eval scores the drafter-critic's covered set against this: with the Critic loop, coverage should equal the gold (P=R=1.0); without it (the template-first base draft), recall is strictly lower -- the loop's measured value. Elements the evidence cannot support are deliberately absent from the gold and are flagged for human review at runtime, never fabricated.

subjects.note: Full evidentiary picture: sanctioned on-chain exposure + all mechanisms. Every rubric element is coverable.
Matches the Iran advisory (why via advisory) plus on-chain exposure; fully coverable.
The 'internal account' red herring: sparse direct evidence. No sanctioned exposure and no advisory, so why / how / on_chain_evidence are NOT coverable and are flagged for human review.
Peripheral account: no anomalies (no 'what'), no exposure, no advisory. Only identity, timeframe, geography, and network-context are coverable.
